title: Prose-Gate Registry Schema
description: Validates .claude/patterns/prose-gates.json. Each gate binds prose intent in a skill state file to a programmatic enforcement mechanism.

gate_id.description: Stable identifier; matches the <!-- prose-gate:<id> --> annotation.

skill.description: Skill name (e.g., 'verify') or '*' for cross-skill gates.

state_id.description: State id (e.g., '3a', '6', '99') or '*' for cross-state gates.

intent.description: Human-readable prose describing what the gate enforces.

enforcer.description: Path to the hook or script that enforces the gate at write/spawn time.

validator.description: Path to the validator script invoked by hook or VERIFY.

required_artifact.description: Path to the artifact that satisfies the gate (when enforcement_kind=artifact-schema).

required_fields.description: Per-field validation rules (min_length, enum, type, pattern, within_seconds_of_now, non_empty_when).

required_skill_yaml_field.description: Dotted field path inside skill.yaml (when enforcement_kind=skill-yaml-mode).

bound_semantics.description: Per-agent bound rules for lead-synthesized numerical claims. Keys are agent names; '_default' is the defense-in-depth fallback.

waivers.description: Sanctioned exemptions where the gate would otherwise fire.

deviation_log.description: When true, violations append to .runs/lead-deviation-log.jsonl with gate_layer:prose-gates-v1.

fail_mode.description: warn = log and allow (Phase A/B); deny = block (Phase C).

retrospective_visible.description: When true, deviations surface in retrospective-enumerator's 6th channel.

prose_locations.description: File:line references where the prose intent lives.
